title: GRIP beam_crossing
description: A single beam-break timing event reported by a trackside gate node (GRIP-Event-Beam). One car crossing one beam produces one of these. The base correlates events from start and finish gates by device and UTC time to compute section times.

v.description: Schema version of this message shape (GRIP_MSG_SCHEMA_VERSION in firmware).

device.description: Node identity, e.g. 'start-gate-01' or 'finish-gate-01'. Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens; this charset is what lets the firmware embed it in JSON without escaping.

beam.description: Beam index on the node. 0 for a single-beam gate.

seq.description: Per-node sequence number, monotonically increasing from 1. Lets the base detect dropped reports over a lossy link.

t_utc_us.description: Capture time in UTC, unix microseconds. Meaningful only when time_valid is true; otherwise a best-effort placeholder.

t_mono_us.description: Raw monotonic capture time (microseconds since node boot). Always present, even before GPS lock, so the base can reconcile late-disciplined events.

time_valid.description: True when t_utc_us is disciplined to GPS time (PPS + NMEA); false when the node had no fix yet and only the monotonic capture is trustworthy.

fix.description: GNSS fix quality at capture, from NMEA GGA field 6 (0 none, 1 GPS, 2 DGPS, 4 RTK fixed, 5 RTK float).

sats.description: Satellites used at capture, from NMEA GGA field 7.
